leagueBriefing: The Miami Open qualifying draw concluded with a mix of veteran resilience and teenage breakthroughs as the final spots for the Masters 1000 main draw were secured.

games.headline: Buse battles past Wong to secure Miami main draw debut
games.brief: In a grueling three-set encounter, Ignacio Buse showed superior mental toughness to overcome Hong Kong's Coleman Wong. The match was a baseline tug-of-war, but Buse found his rhythm in the deciding set to punch his ticket to the main stage in Miami.
bullets: Ignacio Buse converted 5 of 9 break point opportunities.
Coleman Wong struggled with consistency, hitting 35 unforced errors.
The match lasted 2 hours and 14 minutes of intense baseline rallies.
Buse won 75% of his first-serve points in the final set.
games.context: This victory marks Buse's first successful qualification into an ATP Masters 1000 main draw.

games.headline: Fery outlasts Bu in marathon Miami qualifying final
games.brief: Arthur Fery displayed incredible defensive skills to wear down the hard-hitting Bu Yunchaokete. After splitting the first two sets, Fery managed to break late in the third to avoid a tiebreak and seal a hard-fought victory.
bullets: Fery saved 6 of 8 break points faced during the match.
Bu Yunchaokete fired 10 aces but was undone by critical double faults late in the third.
The third set alone lasted over an hour.
Fery's win percentage on second serves was a decisive 62%.
games.context: Fery continues his upward trajectory in the rankings with this main draw entry.

games.headline: Veteran Hanfmann schools Van Assche in Miami decider
games.brief: Yannick Hanfmann used his veteran experience to dismantle the young Frenchman Luca Van Assche. Hanfmann's heavy groundstrokes pushed Van Assche deep behind the baseline, allowing the German to dictate play throughout the majority of the match.
bullets: Hanfmann won 12 of 15 points at the net.
Van Assche was broken four times in the match.
Hanfmann dominated the first set in just 28 minutes.
The German finished with 28 winners to only 15 unforced errors.
games.context: Hanfmann's experience on the big stage proved vital in securing another Masters 1000 appearance.

games.headline: O'Connell dampens Blockx's Miami hopes with gritty win
games.brief: Christopher O'Connell survived a scare from the highly touted Belgian teenager Alexander Blockx. O'Connell's slice backhand effectively neutralized Blockx's power, forcing the youngster into uncomfortable positions during the critical third-set stretch.
bullets: O'Connell won 82% of his first-serve points.
Blockx squandered a break lead in the second set.
The match featured only three total breaks of serve.
O'Connell's consistency proved the difference in the final set tie-break scenarios.
games.context: The Aussie veteran secures a vital main draw spot to protect his ranking points.

games.headline: Barrios Vera upsets Vukic in straight-sets qualifying shocker
games.brief: Tomas Barrios Vera delivered a clinical performance to oust Aleksandar Vukic. The Chilean was imperious on serve and took advantage of a flat-looking Vukic to secure a surprisingly straightforward path into the main draw.
bullets: Barrios Vera did not face a single break point the entire match.
Vukic committed 4 double faults in the opening set alone.
The Chilean won 90% of his first-serve points.
The match was completed in a swift 78 minutes.
games.context: Barrios Vera's win provides a significant boost to his pursuit of the top 100.

games.headline: Svajda survives Merida Aguilar thriller to reach main draw
games.brief: American Zachary Svajda came through a roller-coaster three-set match against Spain's Daniel Merida Aguilar. After a dominant first set, Svajda had to dig deep in a third-set tiebreak to overcome the spirited Spaniard in front of a supportive home crowd.
bullets: Svajda raced through the first set 6-0 in just 22 minutes.
Merida Aguilar saved two match points in the second set to force a decider.
The third set tiebreak went to 8-6 in Svajda's favor.
Both players finished with exactly 95 points won.
games.context: Svajda maintains the American presence in the Miami main draw with this gritty win.

games.headline: Teen sensation Landaluce stuns Tirante in Miami
games.brief: In a battle of youth vs. experience, 20-year-old Martin Landaluce overcame Argentinian Thiago Agustin Tirante. Landaluce's aggressive return game put Tirante under constant pressure, ultimately prevailing in a high-quality three-set showdown.
bullets: Landaluce broke Tirante's serve four times.
Tirante served 12 aces but struggled with a 55% first-serve percentage.
Landaluce won the final three games of the third set.
This match marked Landaluce's first win over a top-100 opponent this season.
games.context: Landaluce's victory signals his arrival as a serious threat on the ATP Tour.
